applied_optimizations.name: Triton kernel for GELU + Global Average Pooling with tl.math.erf
applied_optimizations.description: Implemented Triton kernel that fuses GELU activation (using tl.math.erf for exact PyTorch match) and global average pooling. BLOCK_SIZE=4096 provides good balance between register pressure and loop overhead.

applied_optimizations.name: Fuse bias addition into Triton kernel
applied_optimizations.description: Modified Conv2d to use bias=False and added bias as separate parameter. The Triton kernel now loads bias, adds it to conv output, then applies GELU and pooling. This eliminates the separate bias add kernel (0.77ms) and its associated memory round-trip.

attempted_but_failed.name: Tanh-based GELU approximation
attempted_but_failed.reason: PyTorch's F.gelu() uses erf-based formula, not tanh approximation. The tanh approximation produced max diff of 0.000128 which exceeded the 1e-4 tolerance.

attempted_but_failed.name: BLOCK_SIZE tuning beyond 4096
attempted_but_failed.reason: Increasing BLOCK_SIZE to 8192 gave slightly worse performance (2.91ms vs 2.90ms), likely due to increased register pressure reducing occupancy.

top_bottlenecks: Conv2d (miopenSp3AsmConv): ~1.56ms (73% of runtime) - dominates but not optimizable with Triton in this task
Triton fused kernel (bias+GELU+pool): ~0.57ms (27% of runtime) - successfully fused 3 operations

notes: Key finding 1: Must use tl.math.erf for GELU to match PyTorch's exact formula
Key finding 2: Fusing bias add into the Triton kernel eliminated 0.77ms (23% of total runtime)
BLOCK_SIZE=4096 gives best performance with 16 iterations per block
Final result: 2.13ms vs 3.37ms baseline = 1.58x speedup, score 65.8
The Conv2d operation (73% of runtime) is the remaining bottleneck but cannot be optimized with Triton in this task scope

attempted_but_failed.name: trial_4
attempted_but_failed.reason: Trials 2, 3, and 4 all produced zero output — the agent keeps crashing or timing out. The working solution from trial 1 (score 65.90) is still the best. Need extremely minimal, step-by-step instructio
